TimeEntry.Resource: R. El Khoury IRV-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Internet installation
TimeEntry.Notes: Called support again and work with them to add the static IP address in modem. After that updated the status to client.

TimeEntry.Notes: After finishing updates on a Dell Latitude 5520 (BHHKKG3), an error message kept popping up, "power surge on USB port". Clicked on error message, then Reset. Didn't work. Deleted USB controllers fr

TimeEntry.Title: BRM - reimage laptops

TimeEntry.Notes: Went to Bromic with Kraig to assist in provisioning a laptop. As per instructions, powered on the PC, tapped the windows key 5 times, process times out after 10 minutes. Verified internet connectivity

TimeEntry.Notes: Two of the laptops that had previously been prepped for InTune enrollment failed when attempting the final steps on site. Bromic was possibly experiencing intermittent server issues at the time, which

TimeEntry.Title: New Laptop setups
TimeEntry.Notes: Setup 2 laptops on site with autopilot to domain. 2 Errored out brought back to our office for troubleshooting. Left laptop behind to access resources. Troubleshot and emailed Dean. Bad drive in serve

TimeEntry.Title: New Laptop setups
TimeEntry.Notes: Onsite to attempt another onboarding erroring again on both laptops. Wiped them, updated bios and will try again later.

TimeEntry.Title: Link Speed Issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
remote into Kraig's Computer and Checked the Link speed as per Dean's email. On Cisco, the ports show connected and negotiated at 1G/Full Duplex.
Checked ESXif host and verified the NIC is con

TimeEntry.Title: Prep laptops for InTune enrollment
TimeEntry.Notes: Prepping a Dell Latitude 7440 (4NCDRW3) and HP Envy x360 (8CG3401G41) for Intune Enrollment. Logged into Bromic Office365 as admin. Endpoint Security>Devices>Windows>Windows Enrollm

TimeEntry.Title: Provisioning
TimeEntry.Notes: Took HP Envy x360 (8CG3401G41) and Dell Latitude 7440 (4NCDRW3) to Bromic to provision. After more than 20 minutes on the provisioning screen, the HP errored out, error: 0x80180022. Reimaged

TimeEntry.Title: Prep laptops for InTune enrollment
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into Bromic Office365 as admin. Endpoint Security>Devices>Windows>Windows Enrollment>Devices and searched for serial number of HP Envy. Reimaged it again. Connected to LAN ran Power

TimeEntry.Title: Provisioning
TimeEntry.Notes: Took the HP Envy x360 (8CG3401G41) back to Bromic to provision. After more than 30 minutes of "Pre-Provisioning" the PC failed autopilot again. Discovered that this PC has Windows Home installed

TimeEntry.Title: Setting a user laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: We received an email from the user reporting difficulty setting up their new computer. An error message indicated the need to connect to Wi-Fi first, with the next option grayed out. Upon calling the

TimeEntry.Title: Setting a user laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Scott Comeaux reports that PC can't connect to internet. This Dell Latitude 7440 (4NCDRW3) was one I recently enrolled. Powered it on and waited. After about a minute, the network icon showed as "Iden

TimeEntry.Title: USB power surge error message
TimeEntry.Notes: Scott Comeaux reports that a "Power surge on the USB port" error message keeps popping up. Brought the PC back to the office. One of the USB ports is not functioning. Discovered that this PC (ST: BHHK

TimeEntry.Title: USB power surge error message
TimeEntry.Notes: Reimaged the Dell Latitude 5520 - installed fresh version of Windows 11 pro. The “Power surge on the USB port” error message persists. As instructed by Dell Support, I replied to the

TimeEntry.Title: USB power surge error message
TimeEntry.Notes: On the phone with Dell support. Had me try different USB devices, uninstall USB Root Hub and reboot, reinstall chipset and reboot. Scheduled an onsite service call for May 1st, between 8am-4pm.

TimeEntry.Title: wipe PC
TimeEntry.Notes: Created win11 multi boot disk wiped pc and updated David

TimeEntry.Title: USB power surge error message
TimeEntry.Notes: Dell service tech from Unisys replaced motherboard laptop. Prepared Dell Latitude 5520 Laptop (BHHKKG3) - Pre-staging for BRM Autopilot enrollment. Logged into Bromic M365 as admin, deleted PC fr

TimeEntry.Title: Windows Home Laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Bromic had purchased a laptop that had the Windows Home OS installed, which is not supported by Autopilot enrollment. I setup the PC and did all the updates, also made sure it had been activated. S

TimeEntry.Title: USB power surge error message
TimeEntry.Notes: Went to finish enrollment in autopilot for the PC, after it being repaired, but the enrollment failed. Tried again and failed. Odd thing is, this PC had been enrolled successfully before. Going to sea

TimeEntry.Title: USB power surge error message
TimeEntry.Notes: Resetting this PC, again. Searching through Microsoft Entra for any traces of this PC that need to be deleted. Ran the powershell commands, according to CMD prompt the device successfully synced, and

TimeEntry.Title: Windows Home Laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Bromic purchased a HP X360 laptop with Windows Home installed, which is not supported by the Bromic Autopilot program. Dean Sekuloski requested we upgrade that laptop to Win11 Pro, so I installed a fr

TimeEntry.Title: Windows Home Laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Finally! Both laptops provisioned successfully. Dell Latitude 5220 - BHHKKG3 - Intune device: BRO-ntZOzumzqqh and HP Envy x360 - 8CG3401G41 - Intune device: BRO-A0Uktl0NlpD. The Dell fr

TimeEntry.Title: Reseal New Laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: 4 new Dell Precision 3480 laptops arrived for Bromic today. Was asked to provision one (4GH0WW3) to be shipped as soon as possible to Joshua Murray in Beaumont, CA. Performed all the prestaging PowerS

TimeEntry.Title: Reseal New Laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Received an email from Dean Sekuloski that he would prefer us to login as the user (Joshua Murray) and finish setup before shipping out this laptop. Sent credentials and instructions for logging into

TimeEntry.Title: Reseal New Laptop

TimeEntry.Title: Reseal New Laptop
TimeEntry.Notes: Dean Sekuloski requested a laptop be resealed immediately. Started the process, ran the commands. This will be the first time resealing without having to go onsite to finish.
